test_description: This test case evaluates the generator model's accuracy and technical depth, assuming a perfect retrieval process.
question: [Choice] In hybrid bonding of semiconductor wafers, which combination of metal pad recess depth conditions most effectively ensures a robust, seamless copper-to-copper bond during low-temperature annealing? A) Both top and bottom metal pads recessed less than or equal to 10 Angstroms B) Top pad recess depth in Angstroms plus 20 less than bottom pad recess depth in Angstroms C) Either A or B D) Neither A nor B

gold_context_sources.section_title: US8802538: Method for hybrid bonding of semiconductor wafers with metal pad profile matching
gold_context_sources.evidence: By use of the embodiments, sample substrates were sorted into groups based on the amount of dishing or protrusion measured at the metal pads using atomic force microscopy (AFM) and pairs of top and bottom substrates were bonded. ... One embodiment of a profile match parameter is that the top depth TD in Angstroms, plus 20, is less than the bottom depth BD in Angstroms. ... Another embodiment is a recess depth of both top and bottom substrates TD and BD less than 10 Angstroms. ... By selecting top and bottom substrates from the groups, successful bonding was performed between group A substrates and between group A and group B substrates. ... The profile matching of the embodiments provides excellent metal pad to metal pad bonds when the metal pads are well matched.

ground_truth_answer: The correct answer is C. Reasoning: The cited source from US8802538 demonstrates that two main profile match criteria enable robust, seamless copper-to-copper hybrid bonding at relatively low annealing temperatures: (1) when both top and bottom metal pads have recess depths less than or equal to 10 Angstroms, and (2) when the top pad recess depth in Angstroms plus 20 is less than the bottom pad recess depth. Both criteria have been experimentally validated by grouping substrates accordingly and achieving strong bonds with minimal visible seams. Therefore, either condition suffices to ensure good bonding quality, making option C the correct choice.
